description.en: Ayurvedic medicinal tree naturally distributed only in Sri Lanka's **Intermediate Zone** (planted trees in all agro-climatic zones). **One of the three components of *Thripala* (Triphala)** — the iconic Ayurvedic three-fruit formulation with *Bulu* (*Terminalia bellirica*) and *Nelli* (*Phyllanthus emblica*). Fruit is used for digestion, detoxification, longevity tonic, and topical wound/skin-condition treatment. Relatively rare in Sri Lanka compared to other Terminalia species; conservation and systematic cultivation are agronomic priorities.

description.quote: The T. chebula trees are naturally distributed only in the Intermediate Zone of Sri Lanka, although planted trees can be seen in all agro-climatic zones. It is relatively rare in Sri Lanka when compared to other Terminalia species. Fruit is used as one of the three components of thripala in Ayurvedic medicine.
